title: Free Canva Real Estate Business Card Templates
excerpt: In the fast-paced world of real estate, first impressions are everything. As agents, we know potential clients form opinions about us within seconds, so making…
content:
In the fast-paced world of real estate, first impressions are everything. As agents, we know potential clients form opinions about us within seconds, so making that first touchpoint impactful is crucial. While digital marketing and social media play significant roles in today’s business landscape, tangible elements like business cards still hold powerful value. That’s why we’ve designed custom Canva business card templates to elevate your professional image and set you apart in a competitive market.

Table of Contents

Why Custom Business Cards Matter in Real Estate
Exclusive Canva Templates for Your Real Estate Business Cards
Features of Our Custom Business Card Designs
Integrating Business Cards with a Digital Strategy
More Branding Tools for Real Estate Agents
Don’t Forget to Show Your Card Design to Your Broker
Download and Use the Templates
That’s A Wrap!

Why Custom Business Cards Matter in Real Estate

Business cards may seem like a small detail, but they play an outsized role in defining your brand’s professionalism and memorability. Handing out a custom business card at an open house or networking event can make you stand out long after your initial meeting. Unlike generic business cards, a well-designed, personalized card reflects your commitment to quality and sets the tone for future interactions.

Studies show that 72% of people form judgments on a company or individual based on the quality of their business cards. In an industry as people-centric as real estate, custom cards can be a game-changer.

Exclusive Canva Templates for Your Real Estate Business Cards

To help real estate professionals like yourself elevate their branding, we are sharing a collection of custom Canva business card templates. These templates cater to various styles—from modern and minimalist to bold and luxurious—ensuring that your personality and brand essence shine through.

Each design is fully customizable within Canva, a platform known for its user-friendly interface that allows even beginners to create professional-looking content. Here’s a glimpse of what you’ll find:

  1. Modern Elegance Template: Sleek lines and neutral tones for a contemporary and professional appearance.
  2. Classic Bold Template: Featuring impactful fonts and strategic color pops that draw attention.
  3. Minimalist Sophistication Template: A clean, understated look with a focus on essential information.
  4. Luxury Touch Template: Rich colors and subtle textures for a premium look.

These templates are tailored to ensure your name, title, contact details, and branding elements are perfectly placed for maximum readability and visual appeal.

Features of Our Custom Business Card Designs

Every design choice we made for these templates was deliberate to reflect professionalism and ease of use:

  • Color Palette: We opted for professional shades like navy blue, deep gray, and gold accents that convey trust and luxury, along with others to help you stand out.
  • High-Quality Imagery: Spaces for high-resolution headshots are included, as a recognizable face helps create a personal connection.
  • Contact Information Layout: Each template arranges contact details in a way that makes them easy to locate and read at a glance, ensuring essential details aren’t missed.

Integrating Business Cards with a Digital Strategy

Business cards aren’t just a physical tool; they can be a gateway to your digital presence. Our templates include optional spaces for QR codes, linking directly to your website or social media platforms. This integration ensures that potential clients can learn more about you with a single scan, bridging the gap between offline interactions and your online portfolio.

Adding digital elements like QR codes helps reinforce your tech-savvy image while providing quick access to listings or client testimonials, giving your card extended utility in the digital age.

More Branding Tools for Real Estate Agents

Your business card is only one part of your overall branding strategy. To create a consistent brand experience, it’s essential to use similar design elements across other materials such as flyers, brochures, and listing sheets. By doing so, you present a cohesive and professional image that clients will recognize and trust.

At eXp Realty and within the Smart Agent Alliance (that’s us!), agents have access to customizable templates for various marketing needs?. This makes it easier to maintain uniform branding across all print and digital assets.

Don’t Forget to Show Your Card Design to Your Broker

Before you print your new business cards, make sure to show your design to your broker. This crucial step ensures that your card meets state rules and regulations for real estate advertising. Brokers can verify that the required information, such as license numbers or brokerage details, is correctly displayed and compliant with local laws. Taking this step helps you avoid potential issues and guarantees that your branding aligns with both legal standards and professional guidelines.

Download and Use the Templates

Getting started with these custom business card templates is simple:

  1. Fill Out The Form Bellow: Submission of the form will automatically send you to the templates.
  2. Sign In to Canva: If you don’t already have an account, you can sign up for free.
  3. Make a Copy: Once you’ve accessed the template, make a copy to customize it without affecting the original design.
  4. Customize Your Template: Use Canva’s intuitive tools to change colors, fonts, and add your personal or brand-specific details.
  5. Download and Print: Once your card is ready, download it in your preferred format (PDF or PNG) and print it at home or through a professional printing service.


That’s A Wrap!

Investing in a personalized business card is an investment in your brand and your future success. By using these carefully designed Canva templates, you’ll present yourself as an organized, detail-oriented professional who goes the extra mile. Your card won’t just be another piece of paper; it’ll be a conversation starter and a memorable touchpoint that potential clients will keep.

So why wait? Elevate your brand, leave a lasting impression, and stand out in the competitive real estate market. Download my custom Canva business card templates today and take the first step in redefining your professional image.

title: LPT vs Douglas Elliman: Which is Best for Realtors in 2025?
excerpt: In this comparison of LPT vs Douglas Elliman, we will break down the key differences in commission structures, income opportunities, and resources that agents need…
content:
In this comparison of LPT vs Douglas Elliman, we will break down the key differences in commission structures, income opportunities, and resources that agents need to consider before deciding which brokerage is the best fit for their career.

Commission Splits and Fees

LPT Realty offers a competitive commission split that ranges from 80/20 to 100/0, allowing agents flexibility in how much of their earnings they keep. There is no franchise or royalty fee, which makes LPT more favorable for agents looking to maximize their income. The production cap is $15K, after which agents keep 100% of their commissions. LPT Realty also offers flexible monthly fee structures, ranging from $500/year plus transaction fees, or agents can opt for $89 or $149 per month, depending on their plan. Transaction fees are $195 before reaching the cap.

Douglas Elliman, on the other hand, has commission splits ranging from 50/50 to 70/30, which is significantly lower than LPT Realty’s highest splits. Additionally, Douglas Elliman charges a 6% franchise/royalty fee, which reduces agents’ overall income. The brokerage does not offer a production cap, meaning agents continue to split their commissions with the brokerage regardless of their earnings. Monthly fees vary by office, and transaction fees are included in the royalty fee structure.

Income Opportunities

LPT Realty offers additional income opportunities through its revenue share model, allowing agents to earn passive income by recruiting new agents. LPT Realty also provides retirement income and willable income paths, making it a more attractive option for agents who want to plan for their long-term financial future.

Douglas Elliman does not offer a revenue share program, retirement income, or willable income path. Agents at Douglas Elliman are limited to commission-based income, which may not be ideal for those looking to build additional wealth through other streams.

Training and Support

LPT Realty does not offer a formal training program, but agents benefit from 24/7 support, giving them access to assistance whenever they need it, a crucial factor for agents who operate in fast-paced markets.

Douglas Elliman provides training that varies by office, which may lead to inconsistencies in the quality and availability of training. Like LPT Realty, Douglas Elliman does not offer 24/7 agent support, meaning agents may need to rely on office resources during regular business hours.

Technology and Resources

LPT Realty focuses on providing agents with streamlined, technology-driven tools to enhance productivity. Agents can access essential resources such as CRM systems and marketing tools to help them manage leads and optimize workflows efficiently.

Douglas Elliman, being a more traditional brokerage, offers strong branding and marketing support, especially when targeting luxury clients. However, it may not offer the same cloud-based efficiency as LPT Realty for agents looking to streamline their business in a modern way.

Agent Satisfaction

LPT Realty holds a Glassdoor rating of 4.3 stars based on 37 reviews, with agents frequently praising the flexibility in commission splits and the income opportunities through the revenue share model.

Douglas Elliman, with a rating of 3.9 stars from 638 reviews, has agents who often appreciate the brand prestige but express concerns over the lower commission splits and lack of long-term financial planning options.

Final Thoughts on LPT vs Douglas Elliman

LPT Realty offers a more agent-friendly fee structure, higher commission splits, and additional income streams through its revenue share model. Its flexibility in fees and long-term financial benefits make it an attractive option for agents focused on maximizing their earnings and planning for the future.

Douglas Elliman, while prestigious and established, may be a better fit for agents working in luxury markets who value brand recognition over income opportunities. However, the lower commission splits and lack of a cap on commissions could limit earning potential.

title: LPT vs The Agency: Which is Best for Realtors in 2025?
excerpt: This comparison of LPT vs The Agency highlights the key differences in commission splits, fees, and income opportunities. Both brokerages cater to different types of…
content:
This comparison of LPT vs The Agency highlights the key differences in commission splits, fees, and income opportunities. Both brokerages cater to different types of agents, offering distinct benefits depending on what agents prioritize in their careers.

Commission Splits and Fees

LPT Realty provides agents with commission splits that range from 80/20 to 100/0, offering flexibility for agents at different levels of production. LPT Realty also does not charge a franchise or royalty fee, allowing agents to keep more of their earnings. The production cap is set at $15K, after which agents keep 100% of their commissions. LPT’s monthly fees are flexible, with options of $0 plus $89/month or $149/month depending on the agent’s chosen plan. Transaction fees are $195 before the cap is reached.

The Agency, on the other hand, has commission splits that range from 70/30 to 90/10. The brokerage charges a 6% franchise fee, which reduces an agent’s overall income. Additionally, The Agency does not offer a production cap, meaning agents will continue to split commissions with the brokerage regardless of their earnings. Monthly fees vary by office, and transaction fees are included within the royalty structure, providing a more predictable cost, but with the absence of a cap, agents at The Agency may see reduced earnings in the long term.

Income Opportunities

LPT Realty offers a revenue share program, allowing agents to earn additional passive income by recruiting other agents to join the brokerage. This model can provide substantial additional income over time, making it an appealing choice for agents interested in team building. LPT also provides retirement and willable income paths, allowing agents to plan for their long-term financial security.

The Agency does not offer a revenue share model, retirement income paths, or willable income options. Agents at The Agency are limited to their commission-based income, which may restrict long-term wealth-building potential.

Training and Support

LPT Realty does not have structured training or 24/7 agent support, which may be a downside for newer agents or those who prefer more hands-on guidance.

The Agency, like LPT, offers training that varies by office. However, there is no 24/7 agent support, meaning agents may need to rely on local office resources or colleagues for assistance outside of business hours. This lack of centralized, consistent support can be a limitation for agents seeking continuous professional development.

Technology and Resources

LPT Realty offers a cloud-based platform that provides agents with CRM systems, transaction management tools, and marketing resources to help streamline their business operations.

The Agency is well-known for its high-end marketing capabilities and strong brand presence, particularly in luxury real estate. However, The Agency’s focus is more on branding and luxury marketing than on providing a modern, cloud-based tech platform like LPT Realty’s.

Agent Satisfaction

LPT Realty has a 4.3-star rating on Glassdoor from 37 reviews, with agents highlighting the income opportunities and flexibility as key benefits.

The Agency has a 4.1-star rating from 72 reviews. Agents often praise the brand’s reputation and the opportunities to work with luxury clientele, but the higher fees and lack of passive income options are noted as potential drawbacks.

Final Thoughts on LPT vs The Agency

LPT Realty offers a more flexible and financially rewarding model for agents looking for lower fees, a production cap, and passive income opportunities through revenue sharing. It’s an excellent choice for agents focused on building long-term wealth and minimizing costs.

The Agency, on the other hand, is a strong choice for agents targeting the luxury market who value a prestigious brand and high-end marketing tools. However, the higher fees and lack of a production cap may not be ideal for all agents, particularly those looking for more income flexibility and support.

For more in-depth comparisons, check out our eXp vs traditional brokerage comparison and online brokerage comparison pages. Explore the advantages each brokerage offers, and don’t forget to see why many agents are turning to eXp Realty for even more opportunities.

title: LPT vs Berkshire Hathaway: Which is Best for Realtors?
excerpt: This comparison between LPT vs Berkshire Hathaway HomeServices will break down the differences in commission splits, fees, income opportunities, and support systems, helping agents decide…
content:
This comparison between LPT vs Berkshire Hathaway HomeServices will break down the differences in commission splits, fees, income opportunities, and support systems, helping agents decide which brokerage aligns with their career goals.

Commission Splits and Fees

LPT Realty offers commission splits ranging from 80/20 to 100/0, providing agents with flexibility based on their performance and preferences. There’s no franchise or royalty fee at LPT Realty, allowing agents to retain a greater portion of their earnings. The production cap is set at $15K, and after reaching this cap, agents keep 100% of their commissions. Monthly fees at LPT Realty are low, starting at $0, plus either an $89 or $149 monthly option depending on the plan an agent selects. Transaction fees before the cap are $195, which keeps costs predictable and manageable.

Berkshire Hathaway, by contrast, offers commission splits between 60/40 and 90/10. However, it charges a 6% franchise fee, which impacts the overall earnings for agents. Additionally, Berkshire Hathaway doesn’t offer a production cap, meaning agents will continue to split their commissions with the brokerage indefinitely. Monthly fees at Berkshire Hathaway vary by office, and transaction fees range from $295 to $625, adding up over time, especially for high-producing agents.

Income Opportunities

LPT Realty stands out with its revenue share model, which allows agents to earn additional passive income by recruiting new agents. This gives agents a great opportunity to boost their earnings while growing their teams. LPT also offers retirement and willable income paths, ensuring long-term financial benefits for agents as they build their careers.

Berkshire Hathaway does not offer a revenue share program, retirement income path, or willable income options. Agents are limited to earning from commissions, which may be a disadvantage for those seeking to grow wealth through multiple streams of income.

Training and Support

LPT Realty does not provide formal training programs, but agents have access to 24/7 agent support, ensuring they have the help they need whenever they need it. This makes LPT Realty a good choice for self-motivated agents who are comfortable learning on their own but still want access to support.

Berkshire Hathaway’s training programs vary by office, meaning the quality and availability of training are not uniform across all locations. Additionally, Berkshire Hathaway does not offer 24/7 support, so agents may have to rely on local resources for assistance outside of regular business hours.

Technology and Resources

LPT Realty offers agents access to modern technology and tools, including CRM systems and marketing platforms, to help them manage their businesses efficiently. LPT’s technology-driven approach gives agents the flexibility they need to succeed in today’s fast-paced real estate market.

Berkshire Hathaway is a more traditional brokerage, but it does provide agents with marketing tools and resources, especially when it comes to leveraging its well-known brand. However, its technology offerings may not be as cutting-edge as LPT’s cloud-based platforms.

Agent Satisfaction

LPT Realty has a Glassdoor rating of 4.3 stars based on 37 reviews, with agents appreciating the low costs, flexibility, and additional income streams offered through the revenue share program.

Berkshire Hathaway has a slightly higher Glassdoor rating of 4.2 stars from 981 reviews. Agents often praise the strong brand reputation and client base, but some express concerns over the ongoing fees and lack of income opportunities beyond commissions.

Final Thoughts on LPT vs Berkshire Hathaway

LPT Realty offers agents more flexibility, lower costs, and the ability to earn passive income through a revenue share model, making it an attractive option for agents focused on building long-term financial stability. The 24/7 agent support and modern technology platforms further enhance its appeal.

Berkshire Hathaway is a well-established brand with strong recognition, which may appeal to agents looking for the backing of a traditional, trusted name in real estate. However, the lack of a production cap, additional income streams, and higher fees might not suit agents who prioritize earning potential and cost efficiency.

title: Fathom vs Redfin: Which Brokerage is Best for Realtors?
excerpt: In this comparison of Fathom Realty and Redfin, the key differences come down to freedom versus structure. Fathom offers agents the opportunity to earn 100%…
content:
In this comparison of Fathom Realty and Redfin, the key differences come down to freedom versus structure. Fathom offers agents the opportunity to earn 100% of their commission with minimal fees, while Redfin delivers a salaried, tech-driven model with a consistent paycheck and built-in leads. These two approaches appeal to very different types of agents, and understanding how each one functions will help you decide which aligns with your long-term goals.

Let’s explore what makes each brokerage unique in terms of income, fees, tech, support, and agent satisfaction—and why so many agents are exploring an entirely new kind of model that blends the best of both.

Commission Splits and Fees

Fathom Realty gives agents full commission ownership. Agents keep 100% of their commissions, and there are no royalties or franchise fees. The annual fee is only $700, and transaction fees range from $165 to $465 depending on the chosen plan. There’s no production cap, no complicated tiers, and no surprise deductions. Errors and omissions insurance is already included, making this one of the most straightforward, cost-efficient structures in the industry.

Redfin’s approach flips the model entirely. Agents are paid a salary, and instead of keeping the bulk of commissions, they receive a portion, typically between 25 and 30 percent of the total commission, while the company keeps 70 to 75 percent. Redfin doesn’t charge monthly or transaction fees, and there are no royalty fees. That sounds appealing from a predictability standpoint, but for most agents, the trade-off is clear: Redfin’s structure delivers less income potential in exchange for stability.

Fathom appeals to agents who want full financial control. Redfin, on the other hand, attracts those who prioritize consistency and prefer not to worry about the ups and downs of commission-based income.

Income Opportunities

Fathom Realty is purely commission-based. There are no additional income streams available—no revenue share, no team-building incentives, and no retirement income path. You earn based on what you sell, and when the deals stop, the income stops. This model can be incredibly rewarding for experienced agents with high production, but there’s no long-term wealth-building structure in place.

Redfin offers salaried income, which includes base pay, benefits, and bonuses tied to customer satisfaction and performance. While this provides some financial predictability, it also caps the income potential. Agents aren’t rewarded for growing a team, building a business, or scaling their volume beyond a certain point. And just like Fathom, there’s no revenue share or willable income path.

Neither model currently supports agents in building wealth beyond closings, which is one of the biggest reasons agents are exploring hybrid brokerages that combine commission income with stock equity, team rewards, and passive revenue streams.

Training and Support

Fathom Realty takes a hands-off approach to agent development. There is no formal training program or 24/7 support system in place. While experienced agents may not need additional education, those just starting out or transitioning into a new market may find the lack of mentorship to be a challenge. Fathom assumes you’re ready to work on your own terms, with minimal guidance or infrastructure.

Redfin offers a more traditional onboarding and training process. New agents participate in a structured four-week training program designed to prepare them for the company’s systems and customer service model. This training is localized and helpful for agents adjusting to new markets. However, support is limited to regular business hours, which can be frustrating during time-sensitive negotiations or urgent client issues.

Agents who value freedom and self-direction tend to feel more comfortable at Fathom, while those who prefer structured onboarding and operational consistency may feel more supported at Redfin, at least in the beginning.

Technology and Resources

Fathom Realty does not offer a proprietary technology platform. Agents are provided with basic tools for transaction management and marketing, but the responsibility of selecting and managing CRM platforms, marketing systems, and lead generation tools falls on the agent. This makes the model ideal for independent agents who prefer to customize their own business tools—but not ideal for those who want a turnkey system out of the box.

Redfin is known for its tech-forward model. The company provides agents with a centralized CRM, automated lead assignments, and high-end marketing support. Agents are not expected to generate their own leads, which is a major plus for those who don’t want to spend time prospecting. However, the trade-off is a significantly lower commission payout per deal and less control over the business-building process.

If you want to own your leads and run your business your way, Fathom is the better fit. If you prefer structure, steady business, and integrated systems—while accepting lower earnings—Redfin makes sense.

Agent Satisfaction

Fathom Realty holds an impressive 4.6-star rating on Glassdoor, based on hundreds of reviews. Agents praise the transparent fee structure, the ability to keep their income, and the flexibility of the model. However, the absence of training and support is a common theme in critical reviews. Many agents enjoy the independence but caution that success at Fathom requires discipline and self-direction.

Redfin has a lower 3.6-star rating on Glassdoor, based on nearly 2,000 reviews. Positive reviews highlight the leads, technology, and the benefits of a salaried structure. But a significant number of agents express frustration with the income cap, the lack of ownership over their pipeline, and a corporate environment that feels more like working for a call center than a business you own. Several reviews point out that the model can feel restrictive for entrepreneurial-minded agents.

Agent satisfaction comes down to personal preference. If you value control, independence, and higher income potential, Fathom is likely a better match. If you want stability, a consistent flow of clients, and minimal administrative burden, Redfin may feel more comfortable—at least in the short term.

Final Thoughts on Fathom vs. Redfin

Fathom Realty is best suited for experienced agents who want maximum control over their income and business operations. The low fees and 100% commission model are incredibly appealing for agents who have systems in place and prefer to operate independently. But for agents who need more training, leads, or support, the model may feel too hands-off.

Redfin offers a structured, salaried model that prioritizes client service and lead flow over agent autonomy. It’s a better fit for agents who want a steady paycheck and value built-in systems over personal branding or long-term income growth. However, for entrepreneurial agents looking to scale or earn beyond their closings, the model feels limiting over time.

If neither of these brokerages offers the balance you’re looking for, you’re not alone—and there’s a better alternative worth exploring.

title: LPT vs Coldwell Banker: Which is Best for Realtors?
excerpt: Choosing between LPT Realty and Coldwell Banker depends on what agents prioritize in their real estate career. Both brokerages have distinct commission models, fees, and…
content:
Choosing between LPT Realty and Coldwell Banker depends on what agents prioritize in their real estate career. Both brokerages have distinct commission models, fees, and opportunities that appeal to different agent needs.

Commission Splits and Fees

LPT Realty offers agents flexible commission splits ranging from 80/20 to 100/0, which is appealing for agents who want to maximize their earnings. Additionally, there are no franchise or royalty fees, meaning agents keep more of their commissions. The production cap at LPT Realty is set at $15K, after which agents receive 100% of their commissions. Monthly fees start at $0, with options of $89 or $149 depending on the chosen plan, and transaction fees are $195 before the cap is reached.

Coldwell Banker, in contrast, offers commission splits from 55/45 to 90/10, which are less favorable for agents compared to LPT’s 100% option. Furthermore, Coldwell Banker charges a 5-6.5% franchise or royalty fee, which cuts into an agent’s overall earnings. Coldwell Banker does not have a production cap, meaning agents are continuously subject to the split and franchise fee. Monthly fees vary by office, ranging from $150 to $179, and transaction fees are included within the royalty fees.

Income Opportunities

LPT Realty offers a revenue share model, which allows agents to earn additional income by recruiting others into the brokerage. This provides agents with an extra income stream that goes beyond traditional commissions. LPT also offers a retirement income path and willable income opportunities, which allow agents to build long-term financial stability.

Coldwell Banker, on the other hand, does not provide revenue share income, retirement income, or willable income paths. This means agents are limited to earning based solely on their commission from property transactions, which could limit long-term financial growth.

Training and Support

LPT Realty offers training options, though it does not provide 24/7 agent support. This can be a disadvantage for agents who prefer having access to resources and guidance at any time.

Coldwell Banker offers training, but similar to LPT, the quality and availability vary by office. They also lack 24/7 agent support, meaning agents may need to rely on their local office for assistance during regular business hours.

Technology and Resources

LPT Realty provides solid technological tools, with a cloud-based platform that allows agents to work efficiently and focus on their business. Agents benefit from the streamlined CRM systems and marketing resources that LPT offers.

Coldwell Banker is also known for investing in technology, especially when it comes to brand reputation and marketing tools. However, the technological resources available may vary depending on the local office, and agents often rely on established systems for transaction management and lead generation.

Agent Satisfaction

LPT Realty holds a Glassdoor rating of 4.3 stars from 37 reviews, with agents often appreciating the flexible commission splits, revenue share model, and supportive culture.

Coldwell Banker has a 4.3-star rating from 2.6K reviews. While many agents commend the brokerage’s brand recognition and long-standing reputation, concerns are often raised about the high fees and less favorable commission splits.

Final Thoughts on LPT vs Coldwell Banker

LPT Realty is a great choice for agents who value higher commission splits, lower fees, and the opportunity to earn passive income through revenue sharing. Its straightforward cost structure and modern technology make it appealing to agents looking for flexibility and growth.

Coldwell Banker’s strong brand and established reputation may appeal to agents seeking a traditional brokerage, but its higher fees, lower splits, and lack of income opportunities beyond commissions may limit its appeal for agents looking to maximize their earnings.

title: LPT vs Redfin: Which Brokerage is Best for Realtors?
excerpt: In this comparison between LPT vs Redfin, agents will find significant differences in commission splits, income opportunities, and the type of support provided by each…
content:
In this comparison between LPT vs Redfin, agents will find significant differences in commission splits, income opportunities, and the type of support provided by each brokerage. Both companies have unique models that cater to different career paths and goals.

Commission Splits and Fees

LPT Realty offers a flexible commission split structure, ranging from 80/20 to 100/0, with no franchise or royalty fee. The production cap is set at $15K, and agents also receive stock bonuses as part of their earnings, although the amount of stock is not specified. LPT Realty’s transaction fees are $195 before agents hit the cap, and their monthly fees vary depending on the agent’s plan, with options like $500/year or $149/month. This fee structure makes LPT Realty a strong contender for agents who want flexibility and ownership opportunities.

Redfin, in contrast, operates with a very different model, offering commission splits that range from 30/70 to 75/25. Redfin agents do not pay a franchise or royalty fee, but they also do not have the same earning potential as LPT Realty agents. Redfin does not offer a production cap, meaning agents are always subject to the split structure. One of the unique aspects of Redfin is that agents receive a salary in addition to commission, but the relatively low splits and lack of additional income opportunities may not be attractive to agents looking to maximize their earnings.

Income Opportunities

LPT Realty stands out for offering agents multiple streams of income, including stock bonuses, a revenue share program, and willable income paths. The revenue share program provides an extra layer of financial stability by allowing agents to earn a percentage of the commission from agents they recruit into the company. This additional income stream is a key advantage for agents seeking to build long-term wealth.

Redfin does not offer a revenue share model, retirement income, or willable income. However, it provides a salary, which offers some stability for agents who prefer a more structured compensation model. While this can be appealing for newer agents or those who prioritize a steady paycheck, the lack of passive income opportunities may not be ideal for experienced agents looking to grow their wealth.

Training and Support

LPT Realty does not offer structured training programs, which can be a disadvantage for newer agents who need more guidance. However, they do provide 24/7 agent support, which ensures agents can get assistance at any time.

Redfin, on the other hand, has a more established training program with four weeks of local training, which can be beneficial for agents learning the ropes. However, there is no 24/7 support, which can be limiting for agents working non-traditional hours or in fast-paced markets.

Technology and Resources

LPT Realty offers agents a range of technological tools to help streamline their business, including CRM systems and marketing platforms, though specific tools are not detailed. The company’s model focuses on giving agents the autonomy to run their businesses with modern technology at their disposal.

Redfin is known for its tech-driven platform and provides agents with access to proprietary tools, lead generation, and a strong online presence. For agents who value technology and automation, Redfin’s platform can be highly appealing, but it comes at the cost of lower earnings potential.

Agent Satisfaction

LPT Realty has a Glassdoor rating of 4.3 stars based on 37 reviews, where agents praise the income opportunities and flexible commission structure. However, some agents express a desire for more training and structured support.

Redfin has a Glassdoor rating of 3.6 stars from 1.9K reviews. While many agents appreciate the salary structure and technology-driven model, others raise concerns about the limited income potential and relatively low commission splits.

Final Thoughts on LPT vs Redfin

LPT Realty provides more income flexibility through its higher commission splits, revenue share program, and stock bonuses. For agents looking for long-term wealth-building opportunities and independence, LPT Realty offers more favorable options.

Redfin, on the other hand, offers a salary model that provides financial stability, along with strong technological tools and leads. However, the lower commission splits and lack of additional income streams may be limiting for agents who want to maximize their earnings.

For more in-depth comparisons, check out our eXp vs traditional brokerage comparison and online brokerage comparison pages. Explore the advantages each brokerage offers, and don’t forget to see why many agents are turning to eXp Realty for even more opportunities.

title: 12 Free Open House Sign-in Sheets: PDF Editable Templates
excerpt: As a real estate professional, capturing visitor information at an open house is more than just a formality—it’s an opportunity to build relationships and convert…
content:
As a real estate professional, capturing visitor information at an open house is more than just a formality—it’s an opportunity to build relationships and convert guests into potential clients. The key to effective follow-up and strategic lead nurturing lies in how you collect and use this data. That’s where these three tailored open house sign-in sheet templates come in handy. Each template is designed with specific purposes in mind, helping you achieve your business goals more efficiently. Let’s explore these options and how to leverage them to enhance your open house strategy.

Download Templates

Table of Contents

Download Templates
Why Open House Sign-in Sheets Are Crucial
1. Comprehensive Sign-in Sheet with Agent Check
2. Simplified Contact Collection Sign-in Sheet
3. Detailed Contact and Real Estate Intentions Form
How to Choose the Right Template for Your Open House
Final Thoughts
Download Sign-in Templates
Join our FREE Team at eXp Realty

Why Open House Sign-in Sheets Are Crucial

An open house sign-in sheet is not just a piece of paper—it’s your gateway to understanding your visitors and creating meaningful connections. These sheets help gather essential information, provide insight into your guests’ real estate needs, and offer an avenue for professional follow-up. However, not all sign-in sheets are created equal. Depending on the type of event and the level of detail you require, the right template can make all the difference.

1. Comprehensive Sign-in Sheet with Agent Check

Overview

The first template is a comprehensive form designed for real estate agents who wish to gather basic contact information while also identifying which guests are already working with another agent. This template strikes the perfect balance between collecting fundamental data and understanding guest affiliations.

Sections Included

  • Contact Information: Collects visitor name, phone number, and email.
  • Agent Affiliation: Offers a straightforward “Yes/No” to confirm if guests are already represented by another agent.

Benefits and Use Cases

  • Quick Insight into Agent Status: Knowing whether a visitor is already working with an agent allows you to tailor your interactions and follow-ups accordingly. You can focus your efforts on nurturing leads that are still in need of representation, thereby saving time and resources.
  • Efficient Follow-Up: Having clear records of contact information paired with agent affiliation status helps you prioritize outreach. This ensures that your efforts are directed towards potential clients who might benefit most from your services.

Pro Tips for Maximizing This Template

  • Position this sign-in sheet prominently at the entrance of your open house. Greet guests warmly and politely ask them to fill it out before exploring the property.
  • If a visitor indicates they’re not working with an agent, take the opportunity to offer your expertise and provide information on how you can assist them in their home-buying journey.

2. Simplified Contact Collection Sign-in Sheet

Overview

For those who prefer a more straightforward approach, the simplified sign-in sheet template is a perfect fit. This version focuses solely on collecting basic contact information and is ideal for high-traffic open houses where efficiency is key.

Sections Included

Basic Contact Fields: Name, phone number, and email address.

Benefits and Use Cases

  • Quick and Simple Data Collection: This template is perfect for open houses with a large volume of guests where collecting minimal but essential information is a priority. The straightforward format ensures that visitors can quickly sign in without feeling burdened by extensive forms.
  • High-Volume Open Houses: If you expect a busy open house with numerous attendees, this template allows for a seamless flow without causing congestion at the sign-in area.

Pro Tips for Maximizing This Template

  • Use this template for events with broad appeal or when showcasing a property that might attract a wide range of potential buyers.
  • Follow up with guests within 24-48 hours after the open house to maintain engagement and keep the property fresh in their minds. This simple step can make a significant impact on nurturing leads.

3. Detailed Contact and Real Estate Intentions Form

Overview

The third template is the most detailed of the three, perfect for agents who want to go beyond collecting basic information and gain deeper insights into their guests’ real estate intentions. This template captures a comprehensive range of details that can help you customize your follow-up approach and better understand your guests’ needs.

Sections Included

  • Comprehensive Contact Information: Name, phone number, and email.
  • Agent Affiliation: A “Yes/No” that indicates if the guest is already working with an agent.
  • Real Estate Intentions: Options that allow visitors to specify if they are “Just looking,” “Plan to buy within a year,” “Need to buy now,” or “Have a house to sell.”

Benefits and Use Cases

  • Tailored Follow-Up Strategy: With information on the guest’s buying timeline and current status, you can customize your follow-ups to suit their immediate needs or long-term plans. For example, guests who indicate they need to buy now can be prioritized for immediate contact.
  • Targeted Marketing: This template helps you segment your leads more effectively. For instance, if a guest marks that they’re “Just looking,” you can provide them with market insights and updates to keep them engaged until they’re ready to make a move.
  • Lead Qualification: Understanding who is actively looking for a property versus who is merely browsing allows you to qualify leads and allocate your time and resources more efficiently.

Pro Tips for Maximizing This Template

  • Introduce this sign-in sheet with a brief explanation to encourage guests to fill it out honestly. Let them know that the information helps you offer a more personalized experience.
  • Use the insights gathered to create targeted email campaigns or follow-up calls that resonate with each visitor’s stated intentions.

How to Choose the Right Template for Your Open House

The choice of which sign-in sheet to use largely depends on the nature of your open house and your goals as an agent. Here’s a quick guide to help you decide:

  • Comprehensive Sign-in with Agent Check: Use this when you want to quickly identify potential new clients and understand visitor affiliations.
  • Simplified Contact Collection: Ideal for open houses expecting a large number of guests, where simplicity and speed are essential.
  • Detailed Contact and Intentions Form: Best suited for targeted open houses where understanding the motivations and needs of your guests is key to future engagement.

Final Thoughts

Open house sign-in sheets are more than just a means to collect names and contact information; they are a strategic tool for building relationships, understanding potential clients, and fostering follow-ups that convert leads into sales. By choosing the right template, you can enhance your open house strategy and set the stage for successful interactions. Whether you need a straightforward contact sheet or a detailed form that delves into visitor intentions, these templates provide flexible solutions tailored to your needs.

Embrace these templates to make your next open house more organized, informative, and ultimately, more successful.

Download Sign-in Templates

title: LPT vs Century 21: Which is Best for Realtors?
excerpt: When choosing between LPT vs Century 21, agents must consider key differences in commission structures, fees, and income opportunities, as both brokerages cater to distinct…
content:
When choosing between LPT vs Century 21, agents must consider key differences in commission structures, fees, and income opportunities, as both brokerages cater to distinct career goals and financial strategies.

Commission Splits and Fees

LPT Realty offers commission splits ranging from 80/20 to 100/0, which gives agents flexibility in determining how much of their earnings they want to retain. Importantly, LPT Realty doesn’t charge a franchise or royalty fee, so agents keep more of their income. The production cap at LPT Realty is $15K, after which agents retain 100% of their commissions. Monthly fees at LPT Realty range from $0 (with a yearly plan) to $149/month, and the transaction fee before the cap is $195.

Century 21 offers commission splits ranging from 70/30 to 92/8, which is less favorable compared to LPT’s top-tier splits. Additionally, Century 21 charges an 8% franchise fee, even after the agent reaches their production cap. The production cap at Century 21 is higher, set at $22.5K, and monthly fees vary from $0 to $350. Transaction fees are included in the franchise fees, simplifying the fee structure, but the ongoing franchise fee is a major drawback.

Income Opportunities

LPT Realty stands out with its revenue share program, allowing agents to earn additional income by attracting new agents to the company. LPT also offers retirement income paths and willable income, which provides long-term financial security.

Century 21 does not offer revenue share income, retirement income paths, or willable income. Agents at Century 21 are primarily reliant on their commissions, which may limit income potential compared to LPT Realty’s diverse opportunities.

Training and Support

At LPT Realty, training availability and quality can vary depending on the location, and there is no 24/7 agent support, which could be a drawback for agents needing round-the-clock assistance. However, LPT does offer an extensive support network for agents through its technology platforms.

Century 21 offers training that also varies by office, but agents don’t have access to 24/7 support, which can limit their ability to seek help when needed. While the brand has a good reputation for training, the experience can be inconsistent depending on the office.

Technology and Resources

LPT Realty is a newer brokerage, meaning its technology and resources are built on modern, cloud-based systems. Agents have access to digital tools that streamline transactions, client management, and marketing efforts, making it ideal for those looking to leverage tech for efficiency.

Century 21 offers more traditional technology, such as CRM systems and marketing tools, but the offerings vary by office. Agents may not have access to the same level of cutting-edge resources as those provided by more tech-driven brokerages like LPT Realty.

Agent Satisfaction

LPT Realty holds a Glassdoor rating of 4.3 stars from 37 reviews. Agents highlight the flexibility of commission splits and the income potential through revenue sharing as key benefits.

Century 21 has a Glassdoor rating of 4.2 stars from 1.5K reviews. While agents appreciate the company’s brand recognition and office-level support, they often mention the franchise fees as a downside.

Final Thoughts on LPT vs Century 21

LPT Realty’s flexible commission structure, lack of franchise fees, and additional income streams through revenue share make it a better choice for agents focused on maximizing earnings and building long-term financial security. The technology-driven platform also adds to its appeal for modern agents.

Century 21 remains a strong option for agents who value brand recognition and traditional support, but the higher fees and lack of additional income opportunities make it less attractive compared to LPT Realty.
